Reskin over the `designflow-tracking` `/api/tracking/sample` API | 76 | `src/app/pages/sample_tracking/helpers/sample.status.ts` | **Our code** — frontend mirror of the backend sample status state machine (labels + next-status preview; server is authoritative) | 77 | `src/app/helpers/ag-grid/sample.tracking.config.ts` | **Our code** — Sample Tracking grid column config | 78 | `src/app/helpers/services/tracking.service.ts` | **Our code** — tracking API client; `getProdOrderHeader`/licensing/lead-time plus the `getSamples`/sample CRUD/events/comments/photos/groups/boxes methods | 79 | `Dockerfile` | Build pipeline: Node builder → nginx-brotli runtime | 80 | `cloudbuild.yaml` | Google Cloud Build CI/CD pipeline | 81 | `nginx.conf` | nginx serving config (SPA routing, brotli compression, caching) | 82 | `angular.json` | Angular CLI build config (named `vex` — see §11) | 83 | `package.json` | Dependencies and build scripts | 84 | `dist/` | Build output — **never edit, never commit** | 85 | `node_modules/` | Third-party packages — **never edit** | 86 | `src/assets/` | Static images and icons | 87 | `src/static-data/` | Static mock/lookup data files | 88 | `src/CHANGELOG.md` | Version history | 89 | `docs/` | Developer documentation | 90 91 --- 92 93 ## 4. The Prime Directive 94 95 Our custom code lives here: 96 97 - `src/app/pages/` 98 - `src/app/helpers/` 99 - `src/app/components/` 100 - `src/app/custom-layout/` 101 - `src/environments/` 102 - `docs/` 103 104 **Do not scatter business logic into `src/@vex/`.** The Vex framework provides layout, animation, and nav — it does not know about RFQ, item library, or margin formulas. Keep those concerns inside `src/app/pages/`. 105 106 If you must modify a file outside the above directories, add it to the Core Modification Inventory below. 107 108 --- 109 110 ## 4a. Branch, sync, test, and PR workflow 111 112 All feature work for this sandbox happens on `sandbox-albert`. 113 114 Before adding new code: 115 1. Confirm the local branch is `sandbox-albert`. 116 2. Fetch GitHub. 117 3. Bring the latest `origin/develop` into GitHub's `sandbox-albert` by merging `origin/develop` into local `sandbox-albert`, then pushing `sandbox-albert` to GitHub. 118 4. Pull GitHub's `sandbox-albert` back into local `sandbox-albert`. 119 5. Only then add or change code. 120 121 Branch rules: 122 - Work only on `sandbox-albert`. 123 - Use `develop` only as the source branch for syncing and the PR base. 124 - Never commit to, merge into, or open PRs against `main`. 125 - Do not force-push. 126 127 Quality gate: 128 - Add or update unit tests for every function or behavior you create or change. 129 - Run the relevant frontend tests before committing; prefer the narrowest focused tests plus a TypeScript/build check when templates or services change. 130 131 PR rule: 132 - Commit locally on `sandbox-albert`, then push to `origin/sandbox-albert`. 133 - Keep the GitHub PR for `sandbox-albert` -> `develop` updated after pushing. 134 - If the PR does not exist, create it with `develop` as the base and `sandbox-albert` as the head. 135 136 --- 137 138 ## 5. Core modification inventory 139 140 The `src/@vex/` directory is committed into the repo but treated as upstream framework/layout code. Changes there are intentional only when listed here. 141 142 | File | Change made | Why it was necessary | Risk during upgrades | 143 |---|---|---|---| 144 | `src/app/app-routing.module.ts` | Added `onSameUrlNavigation: 'reload'` to `RouterModule.forRoot` options | Enables clicking the RFQ sidenav item while already on `/apps/rfq` to recreate the component (hard-reset the grid to defaults) | Angular router option; safe for upgrades but verify Angular docs if router API changes | 145 | `src/@vex/layout/sidenav/sidenav-item/sidenav-item.component.ts` | Modified `onClick()` to set `sessionStorage['rfq_hard_reset_pending'] = '1'` when navigating to the current URL (same-URL detection via `this.router.url.split('?')[0]`) | Signals to RFQ component that a hard reset was requested; flag is read by `restoreStateFromSession()` | Vex upgrade will overwrite `onClick()` — re-apply the `rfq_hard_reset_pending` logic | 146 | `src/@vex/layout/toolbar/toolbar-user/toolbar-user.component.ts` | Inject `UserProfileService`; show first name only (split JWT `name` on space, take index 0); call `loadProfile()` on init | Display first name in toolbar; eager-load profile for dropdown | If vex toolbar is upgraded, re-apply injection and `ngOnInit` logic | 147 | `src/@vex/layout/toolbar/toolbar-user/toolbar-user.component.html` | Show photo via `` with `object-cover` when `profile.photoUrl` is set, else `icPerson` icon; bind `(userProfileService.profile$ \| async) as profile` | Render profile avatar in toolbar button | Vex template upgrades will overwrite — re-apply `async` pipe binding and conditional avatar | 148 | `src/@vex/layout/toolbar/toolbar-user/toolbar-user-dropdown/toolbar-user-dropdown.component.ts` | Inject `UserProfileService`; add `getInitials()`, `triggerFileInput()`, `onFileSelected()`, `logout()`, and admin-only `impersonate()` shortcut | Profile card with name/email/avatar/photo upload; lets admins reach Manage Users from the name dropdown | Vex upgrade will overwrite — re-apply injections, profile methods, role decode, and `/apps/admin/users` navigation | 149 | `src/@vex/layout/toolbar/toolbar-user/toolbar-user-dropdown/toolbar-user-dropdown.component.html` | Full replacement with profile header (avatar, name, email, change-photo button, upload spinner), admin-only Impersonate button, and logout footer | New profile card UI replacing placeholder template; exposes impersonation from the top-right name menu | Vex upgrade will overwrite — back up and re-apply | 150 | `src/@vex/layout/toolbar/toolbar-user/toolbar-user-dropdown/toolbar-user-dropdown.component.scss` | Added profile-card styles and impersonation button styling | Style the profile card and admin shortcut | Vex upgrade will overwrite — back up and re-apply | 151 | `src/@vex/layout/toolbar/toolbar.component.ts` | Inject `NotificationService`; expose `unreadCount$`; call `notificationService.startPolling()` in `ngOnInit` | Drive the notification badge count in the toolbar | Vex upgrade will overwrite — re-apply injection, property, and ngOnInit call | 152 | `src/@vex/layout/toolbar/toolbar.component.html` | Wrap notifications button in a relative-positioned div; add `@if (unreadCount$ \| async) as count` badge span (red pill, 99+ cap); wire "Add Task" menu item to `openAssignTaskDialog()` | Show unread count; open task dialog from menu | Vex upgrade will overwrite — re-apply wrapper, badge, and click binding | 153 | `src/@vex/layout/toolbar/toolbar.module.ts` | Add `MatDialogModule` to imports | Required to open `AssignTaskDialogComponent` via `MatDialog` | Vex upgrade will overwrite — re-add import | 154 155 --- 156 157 ## 6. Decision tree 158 159 **Add or change a calculation (RFQ margin, landed cost, freight):** 160 1. Read `docs/rfq-math.md` first — it has all formulas, INCOTERM cost bases, and business context 161 2. Edit `src/app/pages/rfq/helpers/equation.ts` — cost helpers (`getFobTotalCost`, `getMddpTotalCost`, `getPoeTotalCost`, `getWhseTotalCost`, `getDeduction`, `getSellPrice`) are in the bottom half 162 3. `calculateLandedCost()` lives in `rfq.component.ts` (it needs access to `getMasterFiledName`) 163 4. Update `docs/rfq-math.md` if the formula changes 164 5. Do not touch the AG Grid column config in `src/app/helpers/ag-grid/rfq-grid/rfq.grid.config.ts` — it is structural only 165 166 **Add a column to the RFQ grid:** 167 1. Add the column definition in `src/app/helpers/ag-grid/rfq-grid/rfq.grid.config.ts` 168 2. Add the column's `colId` to `rfq.component.ts` → `getMasterFiledName()` lookup 169 3. If the column has a valueSetter that triggers recalculation, add it in `rfq.component.ts` via the pattern used by `fob_cost`, `cbm_pc`, `freight`, etc. 170 4. If the column is in the detail row, also update `src/app/helpers/ag-grid/rfq-grid/rfq.detail.grid.config.ts` 171 5. Do NOT add `aggFunc` to any column unless you fully understand the AG Grid aggregation pipeline behavior (see §11) 172 173 **Add a new API endpoint:** 174 1. Add the method to `src/app/helpers/services/main.service.ts` 175 2. Use `firstValueFrom()` (never `.toPromise()`) when awaiting the Observable 176 3. Use `this.setUserAuth()` for headers on authenticated endpoints; add `this.setUserAuthWithLoader()` to show the global loader 177 4. Update `docs/configuration.md` if the endpoint is controlled by an environment variable 178 179 **Add or change a route:** 180 1. Edit `src/app/app-routing.module.ts` 181 2. Role-protected routes use `canActivate: [RoleGuardService]` and `data: { role: ['admin', ...] }` 182 3. Auth-only routes use `canActivate: [AuthGuardService]` 183 4. Update the navigation menu in `src/app/app.component.ts` if the route should appear in the sidenav 184 185 **Add a new feature module:** 186 1. Create the page directory under `src/app/pages/` 187 2. Register in `app.module.ts` (or use lazy-loading via `app-routing.module.ts`) 188 3. Add column defs in `src/app/helpers/ag-grid/` if using AG Grid 189 4. Add a calculation service in `src/app/pages//helpers/equation.ts` if needed 190 191 **Change authentication or role logic:** 192 1. Auth token management: `src/app/pages/auth/auth.service.ts` 193 2. Route guard logic: `src/app/pages/auth/auth.guard.ts` and `src/app/pages/auth/role-guard.service.ts` 194 3. Role names are strings from the JWT payload — `admin`, `sales`, `designer`, `production`, `sourcing_manager`, `vendor` 195 4. Do not change the idle-timeout in `src/app/pages/auth/idle-timeout.service.ts` without also updating session duration expectations 196 197 **Change admin impersonation:** 198 1. Token swap logic lives in `src/app/helpers/services/impersonation.service.ts` 199 2. Manage Users row buttons live in `src/app/pages/manage_users/manage_users.component.ts` 200 3. Normal users call `startImpersonation(userId)`; visible **Factories & Reps** factory rows call `startImpersonation(factoryId, 'factory')`. Do not depend on AG Grid master-detail expansion for the main factory impersonation path; some views expose no expand affordance. 201 4. Nested rep rows, if shown, call `startImpersonation(vendorId, 'vendor')` 202 5. The top-right name dropdown shortcut lives in `src/@vex/layout/toolbar/toolbar-user/toolbar-user-dropdown/`; keep it admin-only and update the Core Modification Inventory if that Vex file changes 203 6. Backend behavior is `POST /api/admin/impersonate/:userId`; `{ targetType: 'factory' }` resolves `Factory.id`, and `{ targetType: 'vendor' }` resolves `vendor.vendor_id` 204 205 --- 206 207 ## 7. 8. Data model / custom objects 236 237 | Entity | Identifier | Where defined | Notes | 238 |---|---|---|---| 239 | RFQ Item | `Price Code` (string) | Backend DB | Primary key for the RFQ grid row. Used as AG Grid `getRowId`. Never rename the field — it is referenced as a string literal throughout the codebase. | 240 | RFQ Group | `RFQGroup_name` (string) | Backend DB | Groups rows in the AG Grid via `rowGroup: true` on the `RFQ Group` column. | 241 | Vendor | `Vendor.id` (number) | Backend DB | Used in vendor API calls (`sendPriceReq`, `addingVendor`). | 242 | RFQ item → vendor quotes | `Vendors` array on the master row (capital V) | Backend association, materialized on the grid row | Each element is `{ Vendor: { id, ... }, Price, Note, Status, req_status, quote_id, ... }`. Holds the detail sub-grid rows. Casing matters — `Vendors` (not `vendors`); the Select Factories duplicate guard reads `node.data.Vendors`. Same `Vendor.id` may appear in multiple elements (multiple quotes); see §11 "Same factory twice on one RFQ item". | 243 | Vendor quote response state | `req_status` (number) on each `Vendors` element | Backend; set client-side too | `null` = no response yet, `2` = responded (set when a `Price` is entered), `1` = factory **declined/passed** to quote (set by the "pass" button in `rfq-button.component.ts`). Drives detail-grid row class rules (`empty`/`responded`/`passed`) and the detail-grid default price sort (declined rows sink to the bottom — see §11). | 244 | Re-quote (stale price) flag | `requote_requested` (boolean) on each `Vendors` element | Backend `RFQVendor.requote_requested`; set client-side too | `true` when Sourcing has asked the factory to refresh a stale price. Set via the "Request new quote" detail-grid context menu (internal roles only → `itemService.requestRequote`); cleared when the vendor enters a new price or clicks the **保留价格 - Keep Price** button (`itemService.keepPrice`). Drives the orange price-cell highlight (`requote-requested-cell` cellClassRules, applied to **both** the `quote_price` and legacy `price` colId branches — see §11 colId duality) and the bilingual `报价 - Quoted ` `tooltipValueGetter` in `detail-cell-renderer.component.ts`. | 245 | AG Grid column | `colId` (lowercase_underscore) | `rfq.grid.config.ts` | Stable identifier. `field` is the human-readable/storage name (can contain spaces). Never swap them — they serve different purposes. | 246 | JWT user payload | `role` (string), `userId` | JWT token in localStorage | Decoded by `role-guard.service.ts` using `jwt-decode`. | 247 | Impersonation token | `impersonated_by`, plus target `role` / `user_id` / `factory_id` for vendors | Backend `POST /api/admin/impersonate/:userId` | Frontend stores the original admin token as `admin_token`, replaces `token`, and restores it on Stop. Visible factory rows pass `targetType: 'factory'` with `Factory.id`; rep rows pass `targetType: 'vendor'` with `vendor.vendor_id`; explicit target types avoid numeric ID collisions with normal users. Factory-row tokens may have `email: null`, so frontend code must not assume an impersonated vendor has an email. | 248 | Duty Rate | Object `{ title: string }` or raw number | Backend | Can arrive as an object with a `.title` property OR as a raw numeric string. Always access as `dutyRateRaw?.title ?? dutyRateRaw`. `title` holds the evaluated total; the equation breakdown lives in `rfqItem_duty_rate_equation` (see §11). | 249 | Duty rate equation | `rfqItem_duty_rate_equation` (VARCHAR 500) | Backend `RFQItem` table | Raw 3-term sum (e.g. `"5.3+25+10"`) that breaks the duty rate into MFN + Section 301 + Trump 2025. Mirror lives in the row at `[duty_rate field]_equation` for `mathExprCellEditor` consumption. | 250 | Factory country | `Factory.factory_country` (VARCHAR 255) | Backend `Factory` table | Country where the factory operates. Drives sub-grid FOB Country auto-fill and the Trump 2025 lookup. Backfilled from the most common `vendor_country` linked to each factory at first deploy of the column. | 251 | CBM/pc | Field name: `"CBM/pc"`, colId: `"cbm_pc"` | `rfq.grid.config.ts` | The field name contains a slash — always retrieve via `getMasterFiledName('cbm_pc')`. | 252 | Landed Cost | `landed_cost` colId / `"Landed Cost (LDP port)"` field | `rfq.grid.config.ts` | Computed value; formula in `calculateLandedCost()` in `rfq.component.ts`. | 253 | Price-for-Sales snapshot | `price_sales_snapshots` (JSON map keyed by `*_price_sales` colId) | Backend `RFQItem.rfqItem_price_sales_snapshots` (TEXT) | Per-row snapshot of `{duty_rate, duty_dollar, container_price, at}` captured the moment one of the 8 "Price for Sales" cells is populated; rendered as that cell's hover tooltip. Capture logic in `helpers/price-sales-snapshot.ts`; mirrors the `requested_price_cells` JSON-in-TEXT pattern. | 254 | Sample | `sample_id_pk` (number) | Backend `designflow-tracking` (`sample` table); read via `TrackingService.getSamples` | AG Grid `getRowId`. Fields: origin/direction/status/office_location/quantity/retail_price/fob_cost plus customer shipment fields `customer_id_fk`, `courier`, `tracking_number`; list endpoint also returns derived `primary_photo` and `box_label`, and the frontend decorates rows with `customer_name` from core `/findCustomers`. Status moves go through `POST /sample/events` (never edit `status` directly). Lives in its own backend tables — NOT `itemHeader`. **UI labels:** `direction` is shown as a "Journey" (`Factory → NYC` / `NYC → Factory` / `Ningbo → Customer`) and boxes by route (`Ningbo → NYC` / `NYC → Ningbo`), via `pages/sample_tracking/helpers/sample.status.ts` (`directionLabel`/`boxRouteLabel`) — not raw "inbound/outbound". Frontend status mirror also in that file. | 255 256 ### Sample Tracking Customer and Carrier Quirks 257 258 What changed: 259 Customer-origin sample creation uses the existing core customer list (`itemService.getCustomers()` 260 / `/findCustomers`) with Material autocomplete and stores only `sample.customer_id_fk`. Courier 261 tracking links are built in `sample-cell-renderers.ts`. 262 263 Why: 264 Tracking must not own a duplicate customer table. Carrier sites differ: UPS/FedEx support direct 265 number URLs, Bombino supports `tracking.php?searchselected=1&tracking_no=`, and AAE's 266 ASP.NET tracking page does not support a reliable query-param prefill. 267 268 Future sessions should: 269 Do not add a tracking-local customer table and do not invent an AAE prefill URL unless the live 270 AAE page adds a documented one. See `docs/architecture.md` Sample Tracking Carrier Links. 271 272 --- 273 274 ## 9. Container inventory 275 276 | Container | Purpose | Managed by | Image | 277 |---|---|---|---| 278 | Frontend app | Serves Angular SPA via nginx with brotli | Google Cloud Run | Built by `Dockerfile`; pushed to Google Artifact Registry. Tag: `{REGION}-docker.pkg.dev/{PROJECT_ID}/{REPO}/{ENV}/{APP_NAME}:{BUILD_ID}-{COMMIT_SHA}` | 279 280 The backend API is a separate service and a separate repository — not documented here. 281 282 Substitution variables for Cloud Run (`cloudbuild.yaml`): `_REGION`, `_SERVICE_NAME`, `_APP_ENV`, `_APP_NAME`, `_ARTIFACT_REPO_NAME`, `_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_NAME`, `_PORT`, `_CPU`, `_MEMORY`, `_CONCURRENCY`, `_MAX_INSTANCES`. All are passed at trigger time — none are hardcoded in `cloudbuild.yaml`. 283 284 --- 285 286 ## 10. What to ignore 287 288 Do not index or analyze these paths — they are generated, vendored, or irrelevant to active development: 289 290 ``` 291 dist/ 292 node_modules/ 293 .angular/ 294 coverage/ 295 src/assets/ 296 src/static-data/ 297 ``` 298 299 The following directories exist in `src/app/pages/` but are legacy/demo scaffolding from the Vex template and are not actively developed: 300 - `src/app/pages/apps/` (chat, mail, social, contacts, scrumboard, help-center) 301 - `src/app/pages/ui/` (component showcase) 302 - `src/app/pages/documentation/` 303 - `src/app/pages/pages/` (pricing, faq, guides, invoice) 304 305 --- 306 307 ## 11. Idiosyncratic decisions 308 309 ### Angular project name is "vex" 310 311 **Looks like:** The wrong project name is configured — `angular.json` names the project `vex`. 312 313 **Actually:** Designflow was bootstrapped from the commercial Vex Angular template. The CLI project name `vex` is the template's name and has never been changed. 314 315 **Why:** Renaming an Angular CLI project requires updating `angular.json`, `package.json`, `tsconfig.json`, and several other files. The risk of breakage during rename outweighs the cosmetic benefit. 316 317 **Do not change because:** Build scripts in `package.json` (`ng serve`, `ng build`) rely on the `vex` project name implicitly through Angular CLI defaults. Renaming without updating every reference would break the build. 318 319 --- 320 321 ### `postSortRows` instead of `aggFunc` for group-row ordering 322 323 **Looks like:** Date Modified uses a custom comparator and `postSortRows` hook but no `aggFunc` — looks like incomplete sorting implementation. 324 325 **Actually:** This is deliberate. Adding `aggFunc` to ANY column activates AG Grid Enterprise's full aggregation pipeline for all group rows, causing `params.data` to be `undefined` in every `valueGetter` and `valueSetter` callback on group rows. 326 327 **Why:** Adding `aggFunc: 'max'` to Date Modified (commit `43db88d`) broke all landed-cost and margin calculations because group rows suddenly had no `params.data`. Removed in `0a2f4f7`. 328 329 **Do not change because:** Restoring `aggFunc` on any column will silently break every calculation that reads `params.data` in a valueSetter. 330 331 --- 332 333 ### `getMasterFiledName` (typo preserved intentionally) 334 335 **Looks like:** A typo — should be `getMasterFieldName`. 336 337 **Actually:** The function is named `getMasterFiledName` (not `FieldName`) across `rfq.component.ts`, `equation.ts`, `detail-cell-renderer.component.ts`, and related files. It is the stable canonical function name used throughout the codebase. 338 339 **Why:** Renaming it now would require touching dozens of call-sites across multiple files, with no functional benefit and real risk of missed replacements. 340 341 **Do not change because:** Renaming half the occurrences would break the build; renaming all of them is a pure cosmetic refactor with zero risk benefit. 342 343 --- 344 345 ### Duty rate arrives as `{ title: string }` OR as a raw number 346 347 **Looks like:** Inconsistent data handling — some places access `duty_rate.title`, others use the value directly. 348 349 **Actually:** The duty rate field is stored on the row as an object `{ title: "7.5" }` when set via the autocomplete editor, but can also be a raw string or number in some data paths. 350 351 **Why:** The backend returns it as an object with a label; older code accessed `.title` directly without guarding. 352 353 **Do not change because:** The guard pattern `dutyRateRaw?.title ?? dutyRateRaw ?? 0` handles both forms. Changing to always-object or always-primitive would require a coordinated backend change. 354 355 --- 356 357 ### `window['itemService']` global references in rfq.component.ts 358 359 **Looks like:** Anti-pattern — using the global `window` object to access an Angular service. 360 361 **Actually:** Some AG Grid callback closures registered in `applyColumnDefinitions()` (which runs before the component is fully initialized) cannot close over `this` because the method that creates them is called from a context where `this` bindings differ. 362 363 **Why:** This is a known tech-debt pattern from the original codebase. The `window['equation']`, `window['colHeaders']`, and `window['_snackBar']` globals inside `container_price` were cleaned up in commit `8fa013a`. The remaining `window['itemService']` references in toolbar button callbacks have the same root cause. 364 365 **Do not change without understanding:** Moving these calls to `this.itemService` will throw `Cannot read property of undefined` if the closure is created before Angular DI is complete. 366 367 --- 368 369 ### `rfqEquation`, `prodEquation`, `licensingEquation` are separate injectable services 370 371 **Looks like:** Unnecessary over-engineering — the calculation functions could live directly in the grid components. 372 373 **Actually:** Each tracking module (RFQ, production, licensing) has its own `equation.ts` calculation service. This is intentional separation: the grid component handles AG Grid wiring, the equation service handles all business math. 374 375 **Why:** The calculation chains (8 margin × 2 type = 16 setters) are called from multiple places (direct cell edits, bulk updates, container cost dialog). Keeping them in an injectable service allows them to be called from dialogs as well as the grid component. 376 377 **Do not change because:** Inlining the math into the component would make it impossible to call from dialogs without circular dependencies. 378 379 --- 380 381 ### AG Grid `colId` (stable) vs `field` (display/storage) 382 383 **Looks like:** Redundant — why have both `colId` and `field` on a column definition? 384 385 **Actually:** In `rfq.grid.config.ts`, `field` is the human-readable storage key (e.g. `"CBM/pc"`, `"FOB Cost"`, `"Landed Cost (LDP port)"`). `colId` is the lowercase-underscore stable identifier (`"cbm_pc"`, `"fob_cost"`, `"landed_cost"`). All code that looks up a column by id uses `getMasterFiledName(colId)` to get the field name, then uses the field name to access `params.data[field]`. 386 387 **Why:** AG Grid requires `field` for data binding; the codebase uses `colId` as the stable internal identifier for all programmatic access. 388 389 **Do not change because:** Changing `field` names would break data read/write on every row. Changing `colId` values would break all `getMasterFiledName` lookups. 390 391 --- 392 393 ### Duty rate is stored twice: equation AND evaluated total 394 395 **Looks like:** Redundant storage — the cell has both an equation string (`"5.3+25+10"`) and an evaluated total (`40.3`). 396 397 **Actually:** The grid cell displays the evaluated total (`40.3%`) so margin math in `equation.ts` can `parseFloat(duty_rate.title)`. The raw equation is stored separately so the user sees the breakdown when they click back into the cell (Excel-style). 398 399 **Where it lives:** 400 - `[duty_rate field].title` — the evaluated total as a string. Consumed by all downstream calculations in `equation.ts`. 401 - `rfqItem_duty_rate_equation` — the raw equation (DB column `VARCHAR(500)`). Persisted to the backend. 402 - `[duty_rate field]_equation` — ephemeral mirror that `MathExprCellEditor` reads on edit. Forward-synced from `rfqItem_duty_rate_equation` in the cell renderer; reverse-synced back in the column's `valueSetter`. 403 404 **Why:** The duty rate is a sum of three components (MFN + Section 301 + Trump 2025) and that breakdown is auditable information. Storing only the evaluated total destroys it. Storing only the equation forces every downstream consumer to re-evaluate on every read. 405 406 **Do not change because:** Removing `rfqItem_duty_rate_equation` reverts users to opaque single-number duty rates — they lose the ability to see what made up the 40.3% after the fact. Removing `.title` requires re-evaluating the equation in `equation.ts` on every margin calculation (and `equation.ts` runs hot — 16 margin setters per row edit). 407 408 --- 409 410 ### `async valueSetter` with Promise rejection cancels the cell edit (AG Grid v35) 411 412 **Looks like:** A bug — the cell edit sometimes cancels even when the user typed a valid value. 413 414 **Actually:** AG Grid v35 awaits an async `valueSetter`'s returned Promise. A rejected Promise (caused by any uncaught throw inside the setter) cancels the edit and reverts the cell to its previous value. 415 416 **Why:** `equation.ts` previously accessed `params.column.userProvidedColDef.colId` (removed in AG Grid v35) inside every margin setter, causing a `TypeError` on every edit. Fixed in `daf2bf9`. 417 418 **Do not change because:** Any uncaught exception inside an async valueSetter will silently revert the cell. All new valueSetters must be wrapped in try-catch, and all AG Grid API calls inside them must use current (non-deprecated) APIs. 419 420 --- 421 422 ### RFQ fill handle keeps drag-copy but blocks double-click fill-down 423 424 **Looks like:** The RFQ grid still enables AG Grid's fill handle (`cellSelection.handle.mode = 'fill'`), even though double-clicking that blue handle previously copied a Tech Pack Link down the whole grid. 425 426 **Actually:** Drag-to-copy is intentional and must remain. The dangerous path is only the AG Grid double-click shortcut on `.ag-fill-handle`, which auto-fills down the displayed grid range. AG Grid v35.1 docs expose fill-handle on/off via `cellSelection.handle`, but no documented option to disable only the double-click shortcut. 427 428 **Why:** A designer accidentally double-clicked the Tech Pack Link fill handle and destroyed values down the grid. The fix in `rfq.component.ts` keeps drag fill enabled and installs a capture-phase `dblclick` listener that blocks events whose target is inside `.ag-fill-handle`. 429 430 **Future sessions should:** Do not set `suppressFillHandle: true` globally or remove `cellSelection.handle` just to stop double-click fill-down; that also breaks desired drag-to-copy. Verify with `rfq.component.spec.ts` tests for drag-fill config and the double-click blocker. 431 432 --- 433 434 ### "Price for Sales" cells use a native-title tooltip via a cell renderer, not the shared `customTooltip` 435 436 **Looks like:** Inconsistent — the 8 `*_price_sales` columns render through `priceSalesSnapshotRenderer` and set a plain browser `title`, while every other column uses the styled `CustomTooltipComponent`. 437 438 **Actually:** Those 8 columns must show their duty-rate/container-price snapshot tooltip **always on hover**. The grid sets `tooltipShowMode: 'whenTruncated'` globally, and AG Grid has **no per-column override** for that mode. 439 440 **Why:** Switching the grid to `'standard'` would change tooltip behavior for every column and the detail grid, and would require reworking the shared `CustomTooltipComponent` truncation gating (high regression risk per the AG Grid incidents in §14). A native `title` set by a dedicated renderer always shows on hover for just these 8 columns, fully isolated. The renderer also clears `tooltipField` on those columns so the AG tooltip does not double up. 441 442 **Do not change because:** Reverting to the shared tooltip silently re-gates these tooltips behind truncation (they would rarely show, since the values are short currency strings). Capture lives in `onCellValueChanged` → `capturePriceSalesSnapshot()`; rendering in `price-sales-snapshot-renderer/`. 443 444 --- 445 446 ### Angular 21 defaults `standalone: true` — module-declared components need `standalone: false` 447 448 **Looks like:** A new `@Component` decorator without `standalone` works in Angular 15 but silently fails in Angular 21 when declared in an `NgModule`. 449 450 **Actually:** Angular 21 changed the `@Component` decorator default from `standalone: false` (Angular 14–16) to `standalone: true`. Any component you add to an `NgModule`'s `declarations` array will throw `NG0302: 'app-xyz' is not a known element` at runtime unless `standalone: false` is explicitly set. 451 452 **Why:** The entire app is still module-based (not standalone). We have not migrated `AppModule`, `RFQModule`, etc. to standalone mode. 453 454 **Do not change because:** Removing `standalone: false` from a module-declared component will break the module at runtime. Add it to every new `@Component` in this codebase until a full standalone migration is planned and coordinated. Example: `FactoryBulkUploadDialogComponent` required `standalone: false`. 455 456 --- 457 458 ### Vendor-facing UI strings are hardcoded bilingual (`中文 - English`), not i18n keys 459 460 **Looks like:** Untranslated, hardcoded strings like `我不想给价格 - Decline`, `保留价格 - Keep Price`, and the `报价 - Quoted ` re-quote tooltip — looks like someone forgot to use the translation framework. 461 462 **Actually:** `@ngx-translate/core` is installed but **not wired** (no `assets/i18n/*.json`, no `TranslateService` init). The vendor portal is used by Chinese factories, so the handful of vendor-facing strings are written inline as `中文 - English` directly in the templates/colDefs. This is the established pattern (`rfq-button.component.html` shipped the Decline button this way first). 463 464 **Do not change because:** "Fixing" one string to a `| translate` key while the framework is uninitialized renders the raw key (e.g. `RFQ.DECLINE`). Either keep the inline bilingual form, or stand up the full ngx-translate pipeline in one coordinated change — don't half-migrate. New vendor-facing copy should follow the `中文 - English` convention. 465 466 --- 467 468 ### Same factory twice on one RFQ item: allowed for the factory, blocked for staff 469 470 **Looks like:** Nothing stops the same factory being attached to an RFQ item twice — there is no DB unique constraint on `(RFQitem, vendor)` and no backend duplicate check, and the detail sub-grid even has an "Add Quote" button that adds a second row for a factory that is already present. 471 472 **Actually:** Two flows with deliberately opposite rules. A **factory** may legitimately submit a second quote line for the same item (different price/MOQ, with a note explaining the difference) via "Add Quote" — that is real data and is allowed. **Staff** (sourcing/production/admin) must NOT add the same factory twice via the "Add Factory" → "Select Factories" dialog. The dialog (`src/app/pages/rfq/vendor/vendor.component.ts`) renders already-attached factories as red, unclickable chips: `toggleSelection()` early-returns for attached IDs, the group "All" checkbox skips them, and the chips get the `vendor-already-added` class. The attached IDs are seeded from `DialogData.vendors`, which `detail-cell-renderer.onBtnNewVendor()` builds from `node.data.Vendors.map(v => v?.Vendor?.id)`. 473 474 **Why:** A factory quoting twice is a valid business event; staff adding the same factory twice is an accident that produces confusing duplicate quote rows. 475 476 **Do not change because:** The guard is **UI-only** — there is no DB or backend constraint behind it, so the Select Factories dialog is the only thing preventing accidental staff-side duplicates. It depends on the master row property being capital `Vendors` with elements shaped `{ Vendor: { id } }`; changing that casing (e.g. to `vendors`) makes `existingVendorIds` always empty and silently disables the guard. The factory-side "Add Quote" path must stay unguarded. 477 478 --- 479 480 ### Detail-grid default sort targets the live price colId, not a hardcoded one 481 482 **Looks like:** `applyDefaultPriceSort()` in `detail-cell-renderer.component.ts` searches `colHeaders` for a column whose `colId` is `'quote_price'` **or** `'price'` (preferring the visible one) before wiring the default sort — looks like defensive over-engineering that could be simplified to a single hardcoded colId. 483 484 **Actually:** The detail grid's columns come from the backend `GridLayout` table (`getGridHeader({ grid_id: 'rfq_detail' })`), not from code. The **live `rfq_detail` layout serves the visible price column under colId `price`** (legacy), while the in-repo config `rfq.detail.grid.config.ts` uses `quote_price`. The two disagree, so the helper must match whichever the layout actually provides. The comparator also reads `node.data.Price` directly (via `isUnpricedOrDeclined()` / `toPrice()`) rather than the cell value, so it is independent of colId/field/valueGetter. 485 486 **Why:** The first implementation (`e8ba0551`) set `sort`/`sortIndex`/`comparator` only on colId `quote_price`. At runtime that column does not exist (the live one is `price`), so the sort silently did nothing and the `Name` column kept owning the sort. Generalized to match either colId in `b241ce79`. 487 488 **Do not change because:** Hardcoding a single colId reintroduces the silent no-op — the sort lands on a column that isn't on screen and `Name` wins. Until the detail grid is migrated to the static `rfq.detail.grid.config.ts` (config-migration is noted as a TODO in `detail-cell-renderer.component.ts`), the backend colId is the source of truth and may be `price`. The bottom-sink rule depends on `req_status == 1` meaning "declined" (see §8). 489 490 --- 491 492 ## 12. Credentials and environment 493 494 All API base URLs are compiled into the Angular bundle at build time via `src/environments/`. There are no runtime `.env` files in the frontend container. 495 496 | Variable | Purpose | Where it lives | Required in dev | Required in prod | 497 |---|---|---|---|---| 498 | API base URL (core) | `environment.url` | `src/environments/environment.*.ts` | yes (localhost:5004) | yes (api.designflow.app) | 499 | API base URL (tracking) | `environment.tracking` | `src/environments/environment.*.ts` | yes | yes | 500 | API base URL (item master) | `environment.item_master` | `src/environments/environment.*.ts` | yes | yes | 501 | API base URL (data sync) | `environment.data_sync` | `src/environments/environment.*.ts` | yes | yes | 502 | `azureClientId` | Azure AD app client ID for SSO | `src/environments/environment.*.ts` | yes | yes | 503 | `azureTenantId` | Azure AD tenant ID for SSO | `src/environments/environment.*.ts` | yes | yes | 504 | JWT token | User auth | `localStorage` at runtime | runtime only | runtime only | 505 | `_REGION` | GCP region for Cloud Run | Cloud Build trigger substitutions | no | CI only | 506 | `_SERVICE_NAME` | Cloud Run service name | Cloud Build trigger substitutions | no | CI only | 507 | `_APP_ENV` | Build environment name | Cloud Build trigger substitutions | no | CI only | 508 | `_ARTIFACT_REPO_NAME` | GCP Artifact Registry repo | Cloud Build trigger substitutions | no | CI only | 509 | `_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_NAME` | GCP service account for deploy | Cloud Build trigger substitutions | no | CI only | 510 511 All environments share one Azure AD application: 512 - `azureClientId`: `8a8695bd-e701-4d30-a5fe-96a2d2cc962b` 513 - `azureTenantId`: `1caeb1c0-a087-4cb9-b046-a5e22404f971` 514 515 The MSAL redirect URIs are built dynamically from `window.location.origin` — they are not hardcoded. For popup SSO, only `/assets/auth-redirect.html` needs to be registered as a SPA redirect URI (not `/login`). All current domains are already registered. See `docs/deployment.md` § Microsoft SSO for the current URI list and how to add a new domain. 516 517 See `docs/configuration.md` for full environment details. 518 519 --- 520 521 ## 13. Deployment 522 523 See `docs/deployment.md` for the full pipeline. Summary: 524 525 - **CI/CD:** Google Cloud Build (`cloudbuild.yaml`) — no GitHub Actions 526 - **Build:** Multi-stage Docker (`Dockerfile`) — Node LTS builds Angular, nginx-brotli serves it 527 - **Registry:** Google Artifact Registry (`{REGION}-docker.pkg.dev/...`) 528 - **Runtime:** Google Cloud Run (managed, serverless) 529 - **Deploy trigger:** Cloud Build trigger on push to the relevant branch 530 - **Rollback:** Cloud Run revision rollback via `gcloud run services update-traffic` 531 - **Environment config:** Compiled into the Angular bundle at build time (no runtime env injection) 532 533 --- 534 535 ## 14. Critical incident log 536 537 ### 2025 — RFQ calculations silently broken by `aggFunc` on Date Modified 538 539 **What happened:** Adding `aggFunc: 'max'` to the Date Modified column (commit `43db88d`) activated AG Grid Enterprise's full aggregation pipeline. All group rows lost `params.data` (became `undefined`) in every valueSetter and valueGetter. 540 541 **Impact:** Landed cost, freight, and all 16 margin calculations silently returned wrong values or threw for any group row. Users saw blank cells. 542 543 **Root cause:** AG Grid Enterprise activates aggregation for the entire grid (not just the column) when any column has an `aggFunc`. Group rows then use aggregated values, not `params.data`. 544 545 **Recovery:** Removed `aggFunc` entirely from `applyAutoFormatters()`. Group ordering is handled by `postSortRows` + `comparator` without needing aggregation (`0a2f4f7`). 546 547 **Rule added:** Never add `aggFunc` to a column in the RFQ grid unless you have confirmed that all valueSetters and valueGetters gracefully handle `params.data === undefined` for group rows. 548 549 --- 550 551 ### 2025 — CBM/pc cell edit always reverted on save (first root cause) 552 553 **What happened:** Any value typed into CBM/pc (or any column calling `recalculate_Lic_Gen`) disappeared after pressing Enter. The AG Grid cell reverted to empty. 554 555 **Root cause (primary):** All 16 margin setter functions in `equation.ts` accessed `params.column.userProvidedColDef.colId`. In AG Grid v35, `userProvidedColDef` is `undefined`, so every call threw a `TypeError`. Because the valueSetter is `async`, AG Grid v35 awaited the rejected Promise and cancelled the edit. 556 557 **Root cause (secondary):** `duty_rate.title` hard access (without optional chaining) threw if `duty_rate` was null.

file.content: # AGENTS.md — Designflow Frontend: Primary Developer Guide Read this file in full before touching any code. It is the authoritative orientation document for any developer or AI session working on this repository. --- ## 1. What this project is Designflow is a product-lifecycle-management (PLM) web application used by sourcing, merchandising, licensing, and production teams to manage the full journey of a product from concept to retail. The core workflows are: **RFQ** (request for quote — collect vendor pricing and compute landed-cost and margin chains), **Item Library** (product master data), **Standardized Products**, **Art Piece** management, and **Production/Licensing Tracking**. This repository is the Angular frontend only; the backend REST API is a separate service at `api.designflow.app`. The business-critical outcome is correct margin and landed-cost calculations in the RFQ grid — errors there directly affect buy prices and retailer quotes. --- ## 2. Multi-model AI note There is no universal ignore-file standard across AI coding tools. `.claudeignore` works for Claude Code. When using any other AI tool, paste this file as your first message and follow the instructions in the "What to ignore" section. --- ## 2a. Documentation map: what to read for each task Always start with this file (`AGENTS.md`). The Prime Directive Our custom code lives here: - `src/app/pages/` - `src/app/helpers/` - `src/app/components/` - `src/app/custom-layout/` - `src/environments/` - `docs/` **Do not scatter business logic into `src/@vex/`.** The Vex framework provides layout, animation, and nav — it does not know about RFQ, item library, or margin formulas. Keep those concerns inside `src/app/pages/`. If you must modify a file outside the above directories, add it to the Core Modification Inventory below. --- ## 4a. Branch, sync, test, and PR workflow All feature work for this sandbox happens on `sandbox-albert`. Before adding new code: 1. Confirm the local branch is `sandbox-albert`. 2. Fetch GitHub. 3. Bring the latest `origin/develop` into GitHub's `sandbox-albert` by merging `origin/develop` into local `sandbox-albert`, then pushing `sandbox-albert` to GitHub. 4. Pull GitHub's `sandbox-albert` back into local `sandbox-albert`. 5. Only then add or change code. Branch rules: - Work only on `sandbox-albert`. - Use `develop` only as the source branch for syncing and the PR base. - Never commit to, merge into, or open PRs against `main`. - Do not force-push. Quality gate: - Add or update unit tests for every function or behavior you create or change. - Run the relevant frontend tests before committing; prefer the narrowest focused tests plus a TypeScript/build check when templates or services change. PR rule: - Commit locally on `sandbox-albert`, then push to `origin/sandbox-albert`. - Keep the GitHub PR for `sandbox-albert` -> `develop` updated after pushing. - If the PR does not exist, create it with `develop` as the base and `sandbox-albert` as the head. --- ## 5. Decision tree **Add or change a calculation (RFQ margin, landed cost, freight):** 1. Read `docs/rfq-math.md` first — it has all formulas, INCOTERM cost bases, and business context 2. Edit `src/app/pages/rfq/helpers/equation.ts` — cost helpers (`getFobTotalCost`, `getMddpTotalCost`, `getPoeTotalCost`, `getWhseTotalCost`, `getDeduction`, `getSellPrice`) are in the bottom half 3. `calculateLandedCost()` lives in `rfq.component.ts` (it needs access to `getMasterFiledName`) 4. Update `docs/rfq-math.md` if the formula changes 5. Do not touch the AG Grid column config in `src/app/helpers/ag-grid/rfq-grid/rfq.grid.config.ts` — it is structural only **Add a column to the RFQ grid:** 1. Add the column definition in `src/app/helpers/ag-grid/rfq-grid/rfq.grid.config.ts` 2. Add the column's `colId` to `rfq.component.ts` → `getMasterFiledName()` lookup 3. If the column has a valueSetter that triggers recalculation, add it in `rfq.component.ts` via the pattern used by `fob_cost`, `cbm_pc`, `freight`, etc. 4. If the column is in the detail row, also update `src/app/helpers/ag-grid/rfq-grid/rfq.detail.grid.config.ts` 5. Do NOT add `aggFunc` to any column unless you fully understand the AG Grid aggregation pipeline behavior (see §11) **Add a new API endpoint:** 1. Add the method to `src/app/helpers/services/main.service.ts` 2. Use `firstValueFrom()` (never `.toPromise()`) when awaiting the Observable 3. Use `this.setUserAuth()` for headers on authenticated endpoints; add `this.setUserAuthWithLoader()` to show the global loader 4. Update `docs/configuration.md` if the endpoint is controlled by an environment variable **Add or change a route:** 1. Edit `src/app/app-routing.module.ts` 2. Role-protected routes use `canActivate: [RoleGuardService]` and `data: { role: ['admin', ...] }` 3. Auth-only routes use `canActivate: [AuthGuardService]` 4. Update the navigation menu in `src/app/app.component.ts` if the route should appear in the sidenav **Add a new feature module:** 1. Create the page directory under `src/app/pages/` 2. Register in `app.module.ts` (or use lazy-loading via `app-routing.module.ts`) 3. Add column defs in `src/app/helpers/ag-grid/` if using AG Grid 4. Add a calculation service in `src/app/pages//helpers/equation.ts` if needed **Change authentication or role logic:** 1. Auth token management: `src/app/pages/auth/auth.service.ts` 2. Route guard logic: `src/app/pages/auth/auth.guard.ts` and `src/app/pages/auth/role-guard.service.ts` 3. Role names are strings from the JWT payload — `admin`, `sales`, `designer`, `production`, `sourcing_manager`, `vendor` 4. Do not change the idle-timeout in `src/app/pages/auth/idle-timeout.service.ts` without also updating session duration expectations **Change admin impersonation:** 1. Token swap logic lives in `src/app/helpers/services/impersonation.service.ts` 2. Manage Users row buttons live in `src/app/pages/manage_users/manage_users.component.ts` 3. Normal users call `startImpersonation(userId)`; visible **Factories & Reps** factory rows call `startImpersonation(factoryId, 'factory')`. Do not depend on AG Grid master-detail expansion for the main factory impersonation path; some views expose no expand affordance. 4. Nested rep rows, if shown, call `startImpersonation(vendorId, 'vendor')` 5. The top-right name dropdown shortcut lives in `src/@vex/layout/toolbar/toolbar-user/toolbar-user-dropdown/`; keep it admin-only and update the Core Modification Inventory if that Vex file changes 6. Backend behavior is `POST /api/admin/impersonate/:userId`; `{ targetType: 'factory' }` resolves `Factory.id`, and `{ targetType: 'vendor' }` resolves `vendor.vendor_id` --- ## 7. Same `Vendor.id` may appear in multiple elements (multiple quotes); see §11 "Same factory twice on one RFQ item". | | Vendor quote response state | `req_status` (number) on each `Vendors` element | Backend; set client-side too | `null` = no response yet, `2` = responded (set when a `Price` is entered), `1` = factory **declined/passed** to quote (set by the "pass" button in `rfq-button.component.ts`). Courier tracking links are built in `sample-cell-renderers.ts`. Why: Tracking must not own a duplicate customer table. Carrier sites differ: UPS/FedEx support direct number URLs, Bombino supports `tracking.php?searchselected=1&tracking_no=`, and AAE's ASP.NET tracking page does not support a reliable query-param prefill. Future sessions should: Do not add a tracking-local customer table and do not invent an AAE prefill URL unless the live AAE page adds a documented one. What to ignore Do not index or analyze these paths — they are generated, vendored, or irrelevant to active development: ``` dist/ node_modules/ .angular/ coverage/ src/assets/ src/static-data/ ``` The following directories exist in `src/app/pages/` but are legacy/demo scaffolding from the Vex template and are not actively developed: - `src/app/pages/apps/` (chat, mail, social, contacts, scrumboard, help-center) - `src/app/pages/ui/` (component showcase) - `src/app/pages/documentation/` - `src/app/pages/pages/` (pricing, faq, guides, invoice) --- ## 11. Idiosyncratic decisions ### Angular project name is "vex" **Looks like:** The wrong project name is configured — `angular.json` names the project `vex`. **Actually:** Designflow was bootstrapped from the commercial Vex Angular template. The CLI project name `vex` is the template's name and has never been changed. **Why:** Renaming an Angular CLI project requires updating `angular.json`, `package.json`, `tsconfig.json`, and several other files. The risk of breakage during rename outweighs the cosmetic benefit. **Do not change because:** Build scripts in `package.json` (`ng serve`, `ng build`) rely on the `vex` project name implicitly through Angular CLI defaults. Renaming without updating every reference would break the build. --- ### `postSortRows` instead of `aggFunc` for group-row ordering **Looks like:** Date Modified uses a custom comparator and `postSortRows` hook but no `aggFunc` — looks like incomplete sorting implementation. **Actually:** This is deliberate. Adding `aggFunc` to ANY column activates AG Grid Enterprise's full aggregation pipeline for all group rows, causing `params.data` to be `undefined` in every `valueGetter` and `valueSetter` callback on group rows. **Why:** Adding `aggFunc: 'max'` to Date Modified (commit `43db88d`) broke all landed-cost and margin calculations because group rows suddenly had no `params.data`. Removed in `0a2f4f7`. **Do not change because:** Restoring `aggFunc` on any column will silently break every calculation that reads `params.data` in a valueSetter. --- ### `getMasterFiledName` (typo preserved intentionally) **Looks like:** A typo — should be `getMasterFieldName`. **Actually:** The function is named `getMasterFiledName` (not `FieldName`) across `rfq.component.ts`, `equation.ts`, `detail-cell-renderer.component.ts`, and related files. It is the stable canonical function name used throughout the codebase. **Why:** Renaming it now would require touching dozens of call-sites across multiple files, with no functional benefit and real risk of missed replacements. **Do not change because:** Renaming half the occurrences would break the build; renaming all of them is a pure cosmetic refactor with zero risk benefit. --- ### Duty rate arrives as `{ title: string }` OR as a raw number **Looks like:** Inconsistent data handling — some places access `duty_rate.title`, others use the value directly. **Actually:** The duty rate field is stored on the row as an object `{ title: "7.5" }` when set via the autocomplete editor, but can also be a raw string or number in some data paths. **Why:** The backend returns it as an object with a label; older code accessed `.title` directly without guarding. **Do not change because:** The guard pattern `dutyRateRaw?.title ?? dutyRateRaw ?? 0` handles both forms. Changing to always-object or always-primitive would require a coordinated backend change. --- ### `window['itemService']` global references in rfq.component.ts **Looks like:** Anti-pattern — using the global `window` object to access an Angular service. **Actually:** Some AG Grid callback closures registered in `applyColumnDefinitions()` (which runs before the component is fully initialized) cannot close over `this` because the method that creates them is called from a context where `this` bindings differ. **Why:** This is a known tech-debt pattern from the original codebase. The `window['equation']`, `window['colHeaders']`, and `window['_snackBar']` globals inside `container_price` were cleaned up in commit `8fa013a`. The remaining `window['itemService']` references in toolbar button callbacks have the same root cause. **Do not change without understanding:** Moving these calls to `this.itemService` will throw `Cannot read property of undefined` if the closure is created before Angular DI is complete. --- ### `rfqEquation`, `prodEquation`, `licensingEquation` are separate injectable services **Looks like:** Unnecessary over-engineering — the calculation functions could live directly in the grid components. **Actually:** Each tracking module (RFQ, production, licensing) has its own `equation.ts` calculation service. This is intentional separation: the grid component handles AG Grid wiring, the equation service handles all business math. **Why:** The calculation chains (8 margin × 2 type = 16 setters) are called from multiple places (direct cell edits, bulk updates, container cost dialog). Keeping them in an injectable service allows them to be called from dialogs as well as the grid component. **Do not change because:** Inlining the math into the component would make it impossible to call from dialogs without circular dependencies. --- ### AG Grid `colId` (stable) vs `field` (display/storage) **Looks like:** Redundant — why have both `colId` and `field` on a column definition? **Actually:** In `rfq.grid.config.ts`, `field` is the human-readable storage key (e.g. `"CBM/pc"`, `"FOB Cost"`, `"Landed Cost (LDP port)"`). `colId` is the lowercase-underscore stable identifier (`"cbm_pc"`, `"fob_cost"`, `"landed_cost"`). All code that looks up a column by id uses `getMasterFiledName(colId)` to get the field name, then uses the field name to access `params.data[field]`. **Why:** AG Grid requires `field` for data binding; the codebase uses `colId` as the stable internal identifier for all programmatic access. **Do not change because:** Changing `field` names would break data read/write on every row. Changing `colId` values would break all `getMasterFiledName` lookups. --- ### Duty rate is stored twice: equation AND evaluated total **Looks like:** Redundant storage — the cell has both an equation string (`"5.3+25+10"`) and an evaluated total (`40.3`). **Actually:** The grid cell displays the evaluated total (`40.3%`) so margin math in `equation.ts` can `parseFloat(duty_rate.title)`. The raw equation is stored separately so the user sees the breakdown when they click back into the cell (Excel-style). **Where it lives:** - `[duty_rate field].title` — the evaluated total as a string. Consumed by all downstream calculations in `equation.ts`. - `rfqItem_duty_rate_equation` — the raw equation (DB column `VARCHAR(500)`). Persisted to the backend. - `[duty_rate field]_equation` — ephemeral mirror that `MathExprCellEditor` reads on edit. Forward-synced from `rfqItem_duty_rate_equation` in the cell renderer; reverse-synced back in the column's `valueSetter`. **Why:** The duty rate is a sum of three components (MFN + Section 301 + Trump 2025) and that breakdown is auditable information. Storing only the evaluated total destroys it. Storing only the equation forces every downstream consumer to re-evaluate on every read. **Do not change because:** Removing `rfqItem_duty_rate_equation` reverts users to opaque single-number duty rates — they lose the ability to see what made up the 40.3% after the fact. Removing `.title` requires re-evaluating the equation in `equation.ts` on every margin calculation (and `equation.ts` runs hot — 16 margin setters per row edit). --- ### `async valueSetter` with Promise rejection cancels the cell edit (AG Grid v35) **Looks like:** A bug — the cell edit sometimes cancels even when the user typed a valid value. **Actually:** AG Grid v35 awaits an async `valueSetter`'s returned Promise. A rejected Promise (caused by any uncaught throw inside the setter) cancels the edit and reverts the cell to its previous value. **Why:** `equation.ts` previously accessed `params.column.userProvidedColDef.colId` (removed in AG Grid v35) inside every margin setter, causing a `TypeError` on every edit. Fixed in `daf2bf9`. **Do not change because:** Any uncaught exception inside an async valueSetter will silently revert the cell. All new valueSetters must be wrapped in try-catch, and all AG Grid API calls inside them must use current (non-deprecated) APIs. --- ### RFQ fill handle keeps drag-copy but blocks double-click fill-down **Looks like:** The RFQ grid still enables AG Grid's fill handle (`cellSelection.handle.mode = 'fill'`), even though double-clicking that blue handle previously copied a Tech Pack Link down the whole grid. **Actually:** Drag-to-copy is intentional and must remain. The dangerous path is only the AG Grid double-click shortcut on `.ag-fill-handle`, which auto-fills down the displayed grid range. AG Grid v35.1 docs expose fill-handle on/off via `cellSelection.handle`, but no documented option to disable only the double-click shortcut. **Why:** A designer accidentally double-clicked the Tech Pack Link fill handle and destroyed values down the grid. The fix in `rfq.component.ts` keeps drag fill enabled and installs a capture-phase `dblclick` listener that blocks events whose target is inside `.ag-fill-handle`. **Future sessions should:** Do not set `suppressFillHandle: true` globally or remove `cellSelection.handle` just to stop double-click fill-down; that also breaks desired drag-to-copy. Verify with `rfq.component.spec.ts` tests for drag-fill config and the double-click blocker. --- ### "Price for Sales" cells use a native-title tooltip via a cell renderer, not the shared `customTooltip` **Looks like:** Inconsistent — the 8 `*_price_sales` columns render through `priceSalesSnapshotRenderer` and set a plain browser `title`, while every other column uses the styled `CustomTooltipComponent`. **Actually:** Those 8 columns must show their duty-rate/container-price snapshot tooltip **always on hover**. The grid sets `tooltipShowMode: 'whenTruncated'` globally, and AG Grid has **no per-column override** for that mode. **Why:** Switching the grid to `'standard'` would change tooltip behavior for every column and the detail grid, and would require reworking the shared `CustomTooltipComponent` truncation gating (high regression risk per the AG Grid incidents in §14). A native `title` set by a dedicated renderer always shows on hover for just these 8 columns, fully isolated. The renderer also clears `tooltipField` on those columns so the AG tooltip does not double up. **Do not change because:** Reverting to the shared tooltip silently re-gates these tooltips behind truncation (they would rarely show, since the values are short currency strings). Capture lives in `onCellValueChanged` → `capturePriceSalesSnapshot()`; rendering in `price-sales-snapshot-renderer/`. --- ### Angular 21 defaults `standalone: true` — module-declared components need `standalone: false` **Looks like:** A new `@Component` decorator without `standalone` works in Angular 15 but silently fails in Angular 21 when declared in an `NgModule`. **Actually:** Angular 21 changed the `@Component` decorator default from `standalone: false` (Angular 14–16) to `standalone: true`. Any component you add to an `NgModule`'s `declarations` array will throw `NG0302: 'app-xyz' is not a known element` at runtime unless `standalone: false` is explicitly set. **Why:** The entire app is still module-based (not standalone). We have not migrated `AppModule`, `RFQModule`, etc. to standalone mode. **Do not change because:** Removing `standalone: false` from a module-declared component will break the module at runtime. Add it to every new `@Component` in this codebase until a full standalone migration is planned and coordinated. Example: `FactoryBulkUploadDialogComponent` required `standalone: false`. --- ### Vendor-facing UI strings are hardcoded bilingual (`中文 - English`), not i18n keys **Looks like:** Untranslated, hardcoded strings like `我不想给价格 - Decline`, `保留价格 - Keep Price`, and the `报价 - Quoted ` re-quote tooltip — looks like someone forgot to use the translation framework. **Actually:** `@ngx-translate/core` is installed but **not wired** (no `assets/i18n/*.json`, no `TranslateService` init). The vendor portal is used by Chinese factories, so the handful of vendor-facing strings are written inline as `中文 - English` directly in the templates/colDefs. This is the established pattern (`rfq-button.component.html` shipped the Decline button this way first). **Do not change because:** "Fixing" one string to a `| translate` key while the framework is uninitialized renders the raw key (e.g. `RFQ.DECLINE`). Either keep the inline bilingual form, or stand up the full ngx-translate pipeline in one coordinated change — don't half-migrate. New vendor-facing copy should follow the `中文 - English` convention. --- ### Same factory twice on one RFQ item: allowed for the factory, blocked for staff **Looks like:** Nothing stops the same factory being attached to an RFQ item twice — there is no DB unique constraint on `(RFQitem, vendor)` and no backend duplicate check, and the detail sub-grid even has an "Add Quote" button that adds a second row for a factory that is already present. **Actually:** Two flows with deliberately opposite rules. A **factory** may legitimately submit a second quote line for the same item (different price/MOQ, with a note explaining the difference) via "Add Quote" — that is real data and is allowed. **Staff** (sourcing/production/admin) must NOT add the same factory twice via the "Add Factory" → "Select Factories" dialog. The dialog (`src/app/pages/rfq/vendor/vendor.component.ts`) renders already-attached factories as red, unclickable chips: `toggleSelection()` early-returns for attached IDs, the group "All" checkbox skips them, and the chips get the `vendor-already-added` class. The attached IDs are seeded from `DialogData.vendors`, which `detail-cell-renderer.onBtnNewVendor()` builds from `node.data.Vendors.map(v => v?.Vendor?.id)`. **Why:** A factory quoting twice is a valid business event; staff adding the same factory twice is an accident that produces confusing duplicate quote rows. **Do not change because:** The guard is **UI-only** — there is no DB or backend constraint behind it, so the Select Factories dialog is the only thing preventing accidental staff-side duplicates. It depends on the master row property being capital `Vendors` with elements shaped `{ Vendor: { id } }`; changing that casing (e.g. to `vendors`) makes `existingVendorIds` always empty and silently disables the guard. The factory-side "Add Quote" path must stay unguarded. --- ### Detail-grid default sort targets the live price colId, not a hardcoded one **Looks like:** `applyDefaultPriceSort()` in `detail-cell-renderer.component.ts` searches `colHeaders` for a column whose `colId` is `'quote_price'` **or** `'price'` (preferring the visible one) before wiring the default sort — looks like defensive over-engineering that could be simplified to a single hardcoded colId. **Actually:** The detail grid's columns come from the backend `GridLayout` table (`getGridHeader({ grid_id: 'rfq_detail' })`), not from code. The **live `rfq_detail` layout serves the visible price column under colId `price`** (legacy), while the in-repo config `rfq.detail.grid.config.ts` uses `quote_price`. The two disagree, so the helper must match whichever the layout actually provides. The comparator also reads `node.data.Price` directly (via `isUnpricedOrDeclined()` / `toPrice()`) rather than the cell value, so it is independent of colId/field/valueGetter. **Why:** The first implementation (`e8ba0551`) set `sort`/`sortIndex`/`comparator` only on colId `quote_price`. At runtime that column does not exist (the live one is `price`), so the sort silently did nothing and the `Name` column kept owning the sort. Generalized to match either colId in `b241ce79`. **Do not change because:** Hardcoding a single colId reintroduces the silent no-op — the sort lands on a column that isn't on screen and `Name` wins. Until the detail grid is migrated to the static `rfq.detail.grid.config.ts` (config-migration is noted as a TODO in `detail-cell-renderer.component.ts`), the backend colId is the source of truth and may be `price`. The bottom-sink rule depends on `req_status == 1` meaning "declined" (see §8). --- ## 12. Credentials and environment All API base URLs are compiled into the Angular bundle at build time via `src/environments/`. There are no runtime `.env` files in the frontend container. | Variable | Purpose | Where it lives | Required in dev | Required in prod | |---|---|---|---|---| | API base URL (core) | `environment.url` | `src/environments/environment.*.ts` | yes (localhost:5004) | yes (api.designflow.app) | | API base URL (tracking) | `environment.tracking` | `src/environments/environment.*.ts` | yes | yes | | API base URL (item master) | `environment.item_master` | `src/environments/environment.*.ts` | yes | yes | | API base URL (data sync) | `environment.data_sync` | `src/environments/environment.*.ts` | yes | yes | | `azureClientId` | Azure AD app client ID for SSO | `src/environments/environment.*.ts` | yes | yes | | `azureTenantId` | Azure AD tenant ID for SSO | `src/environments/environment.*.ts` | yes | yes | | JWT token | User auth | `localStorage` at runtime | runtime only | runtime only | | `_REGION` | GCP region for Cloud Run | Cloud Build trigger substitutions | no | CI only | | `_SERVICE_NAME` | Cloud Run service name | Cloud Build trigger substitutions | no | CI only | | `_APP_ENV` | Build environment name | Cloud Build trigger substitutions | no | CI only | | `_ARTIFACT_REPO_NAME` | GCP Artifact Registry repo | Cloud Build trigger substitutions | no | CI only | | `_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_NAME` | GCP service account for deploy | Cloud Build trigger substitutions | no | CI only | All environments share one Azure AD application: - `azureClientId`: `8a8695bd-e701-4d30-a5fe-96a2d2cc962b` - `azureTenantId`: `1caeb1c0-a087-4cb9-b046-a5e22404f971` The MSAL redirect URIs are built dynamically from `window.location.origin` — they are not hardcoded. For popup SSO, only `/assets/auth-redirect.html` needs to be registered as a SPA redirect URI (not `/login`). All current domains are already registered. See `docs/deployment.md` § Microsoft SSO for the current URI list and how to add a new domain. See `docs/configuration.md` for full environment details. --- ## 13. Deployment See `docs/deployment.md` for the full pipeline. Summary: - **CI/CD:** Google Cloud Build (`cloudbuild.yaml`) — no GitHub Actions - **Build:** Multi-stage Docker (`Dockerfile`) — Node LTS builds Angular, nginx-brotli serves it - **Registry:** Google Artifact Registry (`{REGION}-docker.pkg.dev/...`) - **Runtime:** Google Cloud Run (managed, serverless) - **Deploy trigger:** Cloud Build trigger on push to the relevant branch - **Rollback:** Cloud Run revision rollback via `gcloud run services update-traffic` - **Environment config:** Compiled into the Angular bundle at build time (no runtime env injection) --- ## 14. Critical incident log ### 2025 — RFQ calculations silently broken by `aggFunc` on Date Modified **What happened:** Adding `aggFunc: 'max'` to the Date Modified column (commit `43db88d`) activated AG Grid Enterprise's full aggregation pipeline. All group rows lost `params.data` (became `undefined`) in every valueSetter and valueGetter. **Impact:** Landed cost, freight, and all 16 margin calculations silently returned wrong values or threw for any group row. Users saw blank cells. **Root cause:** AG Grid Enterprise activates aggregation for the entire grid (not just the column) when any column has an `aggFunc`. Group rows then use aggregated values, not `params.data`. **Recovery:** Removed `aggFunc` entirely from `applyAutoFormatters()`. Group ordering is handled by `postSortRows` + `comparator` without needing aggregation (`0a2f4f7`). **Rule added:** Never add `aggFunc` to a column in the RFQ grid unless you have confirmed that all valueSetters and valueGetters gracefully handle `params.data === undefined` for group rows. --- ### 2025 — CBM/pc cell edit always reverted on save (first root cause) **What happened:** Any value typed into CBM/pc (or any column calling `recalculate_Lic_Gen`) disappeared after pressing Enter. The AG Grid cell reverted to empty. **Root cause (primary):** All 16 margin setter functions in `equation.ts` accessed `params.column.userProvidedColDef.colId`. In AG Grid v35, `userProvidedColDef` is `undefined`, so every call threw a `TypeError`. Because the valueSetter is `async`, AG Grid v35 awaited the rejected Promise and cancelled the edit. **Root cause (secondary):** `duty_rate.title` hard access (without optional chaining) threw if `duty_rate` was null.

content.text:
I've completed all required reading (SPEC_V2 in full, the handoff README, CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, the four config files, the RFQ SCSS) and validated the column-group API against the AG-Grid MCP (v35.1.0/angular). Here is my plan — **I have not touched any code.**

## Status so far
- ✅ **Git sync done:** fetched `origin/develop`, fast-forwarded `albert-2sandbox` to include develop's latest (2 commits: feedback-activity-panel, license-tracking, env), pushed to GitHub, local↔remote in sync.
- ✅ Design handoff extracted to `design_handoff_rfq_polish/` in the frontend repo.

## ⚠️ Three findings that contradict the spec — read before approving

**1. The detail sub-grid is server-driven, not config-driven.**
[detail-cell-renderer.component.ts:166](src/app/pages/rfq/detail-cell-renderer/detail-cell-renderer.component.ts:166) loads its columns from `getGridHeader({ grid_id: 'rfq_detail' })` (the backend `GridLayout` table). AGENTS.md §11 confirms editing `rfq.detail.grid.config.ts` has **no live effect** — migrating to that static config is an open TODO. So **Step 4's column reorder + flex-on-note + live colIds cannot be achieved by editing the config file alone.** It needs either a backend `GridLayout` change or a frontend migration of the load path. (The master grid, by contrast, *does* build from the static `rfq.grid.config.ts` at [rfq.component.ts:942](src/app/pages/rfq/rfq.component.ts:942), so Steps 1/3/5 colDef edits there are valid.)

**2. The `sizeColumnsToFit()` call constraint #4 tells me to remove is already commented out** ([line 748](src/app/pages/rfq/detail-cell-renderer/detail-cell-renderer.component.ts:748)). Per AGENTS.md §11, the live price colId is **`price`** (legacy), while config says `quote_price` and the spec says `price`. I'll bind to the live colId defensively rather than trusting any single source.

**3. Frozen identity (§5d) + "update the Default GridLayout record" (§6) require a backend change.** Frontend-only, I can do the `applyColumnState` backfill on `firstDataRendered`; the durable default for new users lives in the backend repo.

## Implementation plan (matches your 5-step sequence; one commit per step, diff shown before each commit)

**Step 1 — Theme + ::ng-deep chrome (zero logic).** Rewrite `themePink` in [grid-themes.config.ts](src/app/helpers/ag-grid/grid-themes.config.ts) with the §4 `withParams` table; rewrite the relevant `::ng-deep` blocks in [rfq.component.scss](src/app/pages/rfq/rfq.component.scss) (header heights 36/48/44, uppercase headers, 2-family group tints replacing the 8-color rules, seam shadow, `.ag-full-width-row` recolor, scenario cell tints, re-quote `#fdeede`, focus border → pink, toolbar radius/dividers). *I'll first confirm the RFQ grid actually binds the `custom`/`themePink` theme before relying on it.*

**Step 2 — Density toggle.** Segmented control in `rfq.component.html` + `setDensity()` in `rfq.component.ts` using `setGridOption('rowHeight')` + `resetRowHeights()`, persisted to `localStorage('rfq:density')`, applied on grid ready.

**Step 3 — Status pill + Step indicator cell renderers.** Two `ICellRendererAngularComp` (with `standalone: false` per AGENTS.md §11), registered as `statusPillRenderer` / `stepIndicatorRenderer` in the grid component's component map, wired onto the `status`/`step` colDefs.

**Step 4 — Detail sub-grid polish.** SCSS polish (§5k) is safe and frontend-only. The structural reorder/flex/colIds depends on the decision below.

**Step 5 — Collapsible scenario groups.** `buildScenarioGroups()` assembled in `rfq.component.ts` (not the config), keeping the 70 flat leaves intact so the existing `.toBe(70)` and per-colId tests stay green; use `groupId` for state persistence and `setColumnGroupOpened(groupId, open)` per group (the README's `setColumnsGroupOpened(null,…)` isn't a real v35 API — MCP-confirmed). Role-matrix test admin (24) vs sales (8).

**Tests:** after each step run `rfq.component.spec.ts`, `rfq.grid.config.spec.ts`, `rfq.detail.grid.config.spec.ts`, plus new tests for `setDensity`, the two renderers, and `buildScenarioGroups`.

Two decisions will change what I build, so before I start Step 1:

questions.question: The detail sub-grid loads columns from the backend (getGridHeader 'rfq_detail'), so editing rfq.detail.grid.config.ts won't change the live grid. How should I handle Step 4's structural changes (reorder, flex-on-note, colIds)?

options.label: SCSS polish only now
options.description: Apply only the §5k visual polish (frontend-only, safe). Defer reorder/flex to a backend GridLayout change or a separate migration. Recommended lowest-risk path.

options.label: Migrate load path in frontend
options.description: Change detail-cell-renderer to build columns from the static rfq.detail.grid.config.ts instead of the backend. Frontend-only but changes a load path AGENTS.md flags as risky.

options.label: I'll change the backend
options.description: You/we update the backend GridLayout 'rfq_detail' record (backend repo is cloned). I do the frontend SCSS now and coordinate the colIds/order with that.

questions.question: Several spec items (frozen-identity default for new users, the 'Default GridLayout' record) need a backend change. What's in scope for this work?

options.label: Frontend only
options.description: Only touch designflow-frontend. For frozen identity I'll do the applyColumnState backfill on firstDataRendered; durable backend defaults are left as a noted follow-up. Recommended.

options.label: Frontend + backend
options.description: I may also modify the designflow-backend repo (GridLayout records) to set durable defaults. Note: that repo's branch sync isn't set up yet and may need its own albert-2sandbox/develop workflow.

content.content:
Your questions have been answered: "The detail sub-grid loads columns from the backend (getGridHeader 'rfq_detail'), so editing rfq.detail.grid.config.ts won't change the live grid. How should I handle Step 4's structural changes (reorder, flex-on-note, colIds)?"="The right model for backend-driven column defs
The backend owns column identity and order. The frontend can apply rendering overrides on top of whatever the backend returns. These are two separate concerns:

What	Owner	Mechanism
Column order, colIds, visibility	Backend (getGridHeader 'rfq_detail')	Backend change
flex, minWidth, type, cellClass	Frontend post-processing	Override map in detail-cell-renderer
sizeColumnsToFit() removal	Frontend	Delete the call
Visual styling (card, chips, action bar)	Frontend	component SCSS
Revised Step 4 — replace the original with this
Step 4: Detail sub-grid — flex sizing, visual polish.

The sub-grid columns come from the backend (getGridHeader 'rfq_detail'), so
rfq.detail.grid.config.ts does NOT control the live grid. Do not rely on it.

4a. BACKEND — column order (one backend change only):
In the endpoint that serves getGridHeader 'rfq_detail', reorder the returned
columns to: name · price · lead_time · cbm_pc · price_terms · fob_country ·
fob_port · note. This is a sort of the returned array, nothing else. Confirm

content.text:
I've hit a second foundational contradiction with the spec — and this one blocks Step 1, so I'm stopping to report rather than guessing.

## 🛑 The Theming API the spec is built on is not actually wired to the RFQ grid

Spec **constraint #1** states: *"This grid uses AG Grid v35's Theming API (`themeQuartz.withParams`). Every color change goes into the `withParams({})` object… CSS variables / `.ag-theme-alpine` overrides do nothing here."*

The live code is the **opposite** of that:

| Evidence | Finding |
|---|---|
| [rfq.component.html:155-157](src/app/pages/rfq/rfq.component.html:155) | Grid renders with `class="ag-theme-alpine"` + `[class]="gridClass"` — the **legacy CSS theme** |
| [rfq.component.ts:218](src/app/pages/rfq/rfq.component.ts:218) `gridOptions` | **No `theme` property** at all |
| [rfq.component.ts:46-47](src/app/pages/rfq/rfq.component.ts:46) | `GRID_THEME_CONFIG` (which holds `themePink`) and `GridThemeEventsService` are **imported but never used** in the RFQ component |
| [rfq.component.ts:381-383](src/app/pages/rfq/rfq.component.ts:381) | Dark mode is done by swapping the CSS class to `ag-theme-alpine-dark` |
| package.json | AG Grid **35.3.0** (Theming API is the default; the legacy `ag-theme-alpine` class without `theme:'legacy'` is effectively a no-op that logs error #239) |

**Consequence:** editing `themePink.withParams` per spec §4 will have **zero visible effect** on the RFQ grid as currently wired — exactly the "silent no-op" failure mode you and AGENTS.md keep warning about. The Theming-API machinery exists in the repo (`themePink`, the theme service, a theme-picker toolbar) but was never connected to this grid; it's running on legacy Alpine CSS.

To make Step 1 actually reskin the grid, I have to **bind a theme** to the `<ag-grid-angular>` (`[theme]="rfqTheme"`) and drop the conflicting `ag-theme-alpine` class — which also means re-implementing the dark-mode toggle (currently a CSS-class swap) as a theme swap. That's a small but real wiring change, not the "pure styling, zero logic" Step 1 advertises. The same applies to the detail sub-grid (§5k wants `themePink` there too — it's also on `ag-theme-alpine`).

How do you want me to resolve this before I start Step 1?

questions.question: The RFQ grid isn't wired to the Theming API (it's on legacy ag-theme-alpine), so spec §4's withParams edits won't render. How should I proceed with Step 1's theming?

options.description: Bind [theme]=themePink and remove the alpine class now; treat dark-mode parity as a noted follow-up. Faster; dark mode may look off until revisited.

options.label: Keep alpine, reskin via CSS
options.description: Leave the legacy theme; implement all colors/typography via ::ng-deep CSS instead of withParams. Violates constraint #1 but matches the grid's current architecture and dark-mode toggle.

content.text: Clear direction: **keep Alpine, reskin via `::ng-deep .ag-theme-alpine` CSS variables + the §5 `::ng-deep` rules — do not touch `grid-themes.config.ts`, no `withParams`.** That resolves the blocker cleanly. Let me record this important decision to memory, then read the toolbar HTML and the grid-config spec so my Step 1 edits land correctly.
