---
name: rfq-grid-uses-legacy-alpine-theme
description: The RFQ grid renders with the legacy ag-theme-alpine CSS theme, NOT the AG Grid Theming API
metadata:
  type: project
---

The designflow-frontend RFQ grid (`src/app/pages/rfq/`) renders with the **legacy `ag-theme-alpine` CSS theme**, not the v35 Theming API — despite the repo containing `themePink`/`GRID_THEME_CONFIG`/`GridThemeEventsService`.

- `rfq.component.html` `<ag-grid-angular>` uses `class="ag-theme-alpine"` + `[class]="gridClass"`; `gridClass` toggles `ag-theme-alpine` / `ag-theme-alpine-dark` for dark mode (`rfq.component.ts` ~line 381-383).
- `gridOptions` (rfq.component.ts ~line 218) has **no `theme` property**. `GRID_THEME_CONFIG` and `GridThemeEventsService` are imported but unused in the RFQ component.
- AG Grid is 35.3.0 (Theming API is the package default; the legacy class without `theme:'legacy'` is effectively a no-op / error #239).

**Why:** matters for the RFQ polish work (June 2026). The handoff SPEC_V2 §4 assumed the grid uses `themeQuartz.withParams` — it does NOT.

**How to apply:** to restyle the RFQ grid, set CSS variables on `::ng-deep .ag-theme-alpine { --ag-*: ... }` in `rfq.component.scss` plus `::ng-deep` rules on real AG Grid classes. Do NOT edit `grid-themes.config.ts` or write `withParams` expecting visible effect. The detail sub-grid is also on `ag-theme-alpine`. Related: [[rfq-detail-grid-is-server-driven]].

---
name: rfq-detail-grid-is-server-driven
description: The RFQ detail (factory-quote) sub-grid loads its columns from the backend, not from rfq.detail.grid.config.ts
metadata:
  type: project
---

The RFQ factory-quote **detail sub-grid columns come from the backend** via `getGridHeader({ grid_id: 'rfq_detail' })` (`detail-cell-renderer.component.ts:166`), NOT from the in-repo `rfq.detail.grid.config.ts`. Editing that config file has no live effect (AGENTS.md §11; migrating the load path is an open TODO). The live price colId is `price` (legacy), while the config says `quote_price`. `sizeColumnsToFit()` in that renderer is already commented out (~line 748).

By contrast, the **master RFQ grid DOES build from the static `rfq.grid.config.ts`** (`rfq.component.ts:942` spreads `RFQ_COLUMN_HEADERS_CONFIG.columnDefs`), so colDef edits there (cellClass, headerGroupClass, pinned) take effect — modulo saved Views overriding order/pinned.

**Why:** matters for the RFQ polish work (June 2026). Agreed hybrid model for the detail grid: backend owns column order/colIds/visibility; frontend applies a colId-keyed override map (flex, minWidth, type, pinned, cellClass) after the backend load, removes sizeColumnsToFit, and does SCSS polish.

**How to apply:** confirm live `rfq_detail` colIds before any backend reorder. Do detail-grid flex/min-width as a frontend override map keyed by the real colIds, not by rewriting the config file. Related: [[rfq-grid-uses-legacy-alpine-theme]].

content.text:
Here's where Step 1 stands. **I have not committed anything** — awaiting your review and a green test run.

## Step 1 implemented — visual reskin (matches your Alpine CSS-variable approach)

**Mechanism:** kept the legacy `ag-theme-alpine` theme, applied §4 tokens as Alpine CSS variables in `::ng-deep .ag-theme-alpine`, plus all §5 `::ng-deep` rules. `grid-themes.config.ts` untouched; no `withParams`; zero logic changes.

Files changed (diff above):
- **[rfq.component.scss](src/app/pages/rfq/rfq.component.scss)** — Alpine CSS vars (accent/bg/hover/selection/header/borders/font); re-quote cell `#fdeede`/`#e8833a` (§5h); 8-color group headers → 2-family blue/violet (§5a) + group-band classes ready for Step 5; header heights 60→48 / 50→44 + uppercase 2-line headers (§5a); row-group band recolor (§5c); cell border → `#e7e9ee`, focus border blue→pink, row-focus pink (§5b/d); frozen-pane seam + cross-seam selection (§5d, visual only); scenario cell tints (§5e); buttons radius/height/shadow + `.toolbar-divider` (§5i).
- **[rfq.grid.config.ts](src/app/helpers/ag-grid/rfq-grid/rfq.grid.config.ts)** — scenario leaves get `cellClass` (`scenario-gen`/`scenario-lic` + `--sales` on the 8 Price-for-Sales leaves) via a single post-process. Count stays 70.
- **[rfq.grid.config.spec.ts](src/app/helpers/ag-grid/rfq-grid/rfq.grid.config.spec.ts)** — 5 new tests for the tint classes.
- **[rfq.component.html](src/app/pages/rfq/rfq.component.html)** — two `.toolbar-divider`s.

**Deliberately deferred (with reasons):**
- §5a 36px group-band row (`:first-child`) → **Step 5**, since that header row doesn't exist until the ColGroupDef migration. Setting it now would wrongly shrink the only header row.
- §5e margin good/warn coloring (`cellClassRules`) → needs value-predicate functions (logic), not a pure tint; will fold into a later step.
- §5b `cell-numeric`/`cell-strong`/`cell-price-code` → require tagging many colDefs; left out to keep Step 1 reviewable.

## Pending before I can call Step 1 done
This is a fresh clone with **no `node_modules`**, so I'm installing dependencies (via `corepack yarn install`, since `corepack enable` needs admin) to run the specs. The install is still running; I'll run `rfq.grid.config`, `rfq.detail.grid.config`, and `rfq.component.spec` as soon as it finishes and report results.

I'll report back automatically when the install completes and tests run. **I won't commit until you approve Step 1.**

Add a Compact/Standard/Comfortable segmented control to the RFQ toolbar that
switches grid row density (SPEC_V2 §5i). Density drives rowHeight only
(30/36/44); header heights remain fixed by ::ng-deep CSS.

- setDensity() uses gridApi.setGridOption('rowHeight') + resetRowHeights()
- preference persisted to localStorage('rfq:density'), read in ngOnInit and
  applied in onGridReady
- defaults to 'standard'; invalid/missing values fall back to 'standard'
- add unit tests for default, DENSITY_MAP, setDensity, persistence, fallback

Add two AG Grid cell renderers for the RFQ master grid (SPEC_V2 §5f/§5g):

- StatusPillRendererComponent (statusPillRenderer): coloured pill + dot for the

- StepIndicatorRendererComponent (stepIndicatorRenderer): coloured dot + short

  table; unknown values shown as-is with a neutral dot.

and wired via cellRenderer on the status/step colDefs. Pure mapping functions
are unit tested alongside agInit/refresh behaviour.

- Merge or switch branches only when the user explicitly asks; normal delivery is PR to `develop`

## Ignore Guidance

Use `.claudeignore`. It matches `.cursorignore` and excludes generated, vendored, cache, Playwright, runtime upload, and large data paths such as `node_modules/`, `coverage/`, `dist/`, `.cache/`, `images/`, `thumbs/`, and `data/hts-rates.json`.

## Allowed Operations

- Allowed: read/edit repo files, run `npm` scripts, use `git add`, `git commit`, and `git push origin sandbox-albert`
- Not allowed by default: force-push, rewrite history, edit `node_modules/`
- SSH is not the normal deployment path. Deployment is Cloud Build -> Docker -> Cloud Run after branch push; use `gcloud` only for explicit rollback or incident triage.

## Tests

```powershell
npm test
npm test -- --testPathPattern="<name>"
```

Run the relevant tests before committing. `start:sandbox-albert` does not run tests before starting `nodemon`.

## Claude-Specific Rules

- For DB columns: model field + `models/db.js` inline migration + `tests/unit/db.migration.test.js` assertion.
- Quote mixed-case Postgres column identifiers in inline migrations.
- Add code to `routes/lib.router.js` and `models/lib.model.js` surgically; they are large legacy files.
- Keep secrets out of `--set-env-vars`; use Secret Manager via `--set-secrets`.

## Commit Style

```text
type(scope): short description
```

Use `fix`, `feat`, `refactor`, `docs`, or `chore`. Keep messages specific.

content.text:
I've finished and pushed Steps 1–4. Before the highest-risk step, here's the state and two decisions.

## Steps 1–4 — committed & pushed to `albert-2sandbox`
| Step | Commit | Status |
|---|---|---|
| 1 Reskin (theme vars, headers, tints, toolbar) | `55bbe86a` | ✅ tests + build green |
| 2 Density toggle | `134f702a` | ✅ |
| 3 Status pill + Step indicator renderers | `f43aa0b5` | ✅ |
| 4 Detail grid flex/override + polish (frontend) | `96602104` | ✅ |

## Two items that are **DB data, not repo code** (can't be done from the repo)
Both turned out to live in the database, not in either codebase:
1. **Detail-grid column reorder (4a)** — order comes from `GridAccessLevel.col_order` rows ([grid.service.js](../designflow-backend/services/grid.service.js) orders by `col_order ASC`). Reordering = an `UPDATE` on those rows. The frontend flex work already removes the wasted gap; reorder is the remaining cosmetic piece.
2. **Frozen-identity default for new users (§5d/§6)** — the "Default GridLayout" pinned state is also a DB record. I did the frontend `applyColumnState` backfill path is *available*, but the durable default is DB data.

Neither is a code change in `designflow-frontend` or `designflow-backend`, so I can't execute them here without DB access (and the spec says don't guess colIds). 

## Step 5 plan (collapsible scenario groups) — the risky one
- `buildScenarioGroups()` assembles 8 `ColGroupDef`s from the flat scenario leaves, spliced in at [rfq.component.ts:2012](src/app/pages/rfq/rfq.component.ts:2012) (after `applyAutoFormatters`), keeping the 70 flat leaves in config so all existing tests stay green.
- Price-for-Sales leaf = always visible (`columnGroupShow: undefined`); Sell/Margin/Royalty = `columnGroupShow: 'open'`; `groupId` set for state persistence; `marryChildren: true`; `headerGroupClass: group-band-gen|lic` (CSS already added in Step 1).
- **Defensive for roles:** sales/designer only have the price_sales leaf after `filterColumnsByRole` — groups include only children that exist (no `{...undefined}`), so a single-child group renders cleanly.
- Uses `setColumnGroupOpened(groupId, …)` (MCP-confirmed v35 API; the README's `setColumnsGroupOpened(null,…)` isn't real). `groupDefaultExpanded`/calm-collapsed default.
- Add the 36px group-band header row CSS (deferred from §5a). Unit-test `buildScenarioGroups` for admin (8 groups, full children) and sales (8 groups, sales-only child) plus group metadata.

This touches the business-critical pricing grid's column assembly, so I want your sign-off on scope before I start.

feat(rfq): collapsible scenario column groups (Step 5)

Migrate the flat Gen/Lic × FOB/POE/WHSE/MDDP scenario leaves into 8 collapsible
ColGroupDefs (SPEC_V2 §1/§5a). The Price-for-Sales leaf is always visible (calm
collapsed default); Sell/Margin/Royalty show only when the group is expanded
(columnGroupShow: 'open').

- buildScenarioGroups() (new helper) runs on the assembled columnDefs after role
  filtering + auto-formatters, so each leaf keeps all its wiring (tint, cellClass,
  formatter, renderer). groupId set so open/closed state persists with saved
  views; marryChildren keeps families together; headerGroupClass drives the
  2-family band tint.
- Role-safe: children removed by role filtering are simply absent — sales/designer
  get 8 single-child (sales-only) groups; sourcing/production/vendor see no
  scenario columns and the column list is returned unchanged.
- groupHeaderHeight: 36 + a two-class CSS rule for the band row height (§5a),
  which only applies when the group row exists.
- The flat config (70 cols) and getMasterFiledName lookups are untouched —
  grouping is applied only to the grid's columnDefs.
- Unit tests cover admin/sales role matrices, group metadata, child visibility,
  colDef preservation, no-op cases, and input immutability.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

content.text:
All five steps are implemented, tested, built, committed, and pushed to `albert-2sandbox`. Here's the final summary.

## ✅ RFQ polish complete — all on `albert-2sandbox`

| # | Step | Commit | Mechanism |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Visual reskin | [`55bbe86a`] | Alpine CSS vars + §5 `::ng-deep` (grid is legacy Alpine, **not** the Theming API) + scenario `cellClass` tints |
| 2 | Density toggle | [`134f702a`] | `setGridOption('rowHeight')` + `resetRowHeights()` + `localStorage` |
| 3 | Status pill + Step indicator | [`f43aa0b5`] | Two `ICellRendererAngularComp` wired to `status`/`step` |
| 4 | Detail sub-grid flex + polish | [`96602104`] | Frontend override map on the backend-driven detail grid; SCSS §5k |
| — | Detail reorder + frozen identity | [`de0939d6`] | `orderDetailColumns()` + `applyColumnState` on `firstDataRendered` (both code-only, per your call) |
| 5 | Collapsible scenario groups | [`0af2c4b0`] | `buildScenarioGroups()` → 8 `ColGroupDef`s, role-safe |

**Verification:** full jest suite **609 tests / 52 suites green**, `sandbox2` AOT build clean (only the pre-existing bundle-budget warning) after each step.

## Constraints honored
- **No `--ag-*` Theming-API `withParams`** — the grid runs on legacy `ag-theme-alpine`, so all color/typography went into `::ng-deep .ag-theme-alpine` CSS vars + structural `::ng-deep` rules. `grid-themes.config.ts` untouched.
- **Appearance only** — no calculation, edit, fill-handle, undo, or `getMasterFiledName` logic changed. The flat 70-column config is intact; grouping is applied only to the grid's `columnDefs`, so all existing tests (incl. the `.toBe(70)` and per-colId checks) still pass.
- **Frozen identity** done via `applyColumnState` (wins over saved Views), with a `rfq:unpin-identity` opt-out.
- Every new function has unit tests; AG-Grid APIs were confirmed against the MCP (v35.1.0/angular).

## Worth knowing
- **Git workflow ran throughout:** synced `develop` → `albert-2sandbox` first; each step rebased onto the latest remote before pushing (the branch received external commits twice mid-work). Commits are authored as Albert Hazan (your local git identity).
- **Branch discrepancy:** repo `CLAUDE.md`/`AGENTS.md` name `sandbox-albert`; I used `albert-2sandbox` per your explicit instruction. Flagging in case the docs need updating.
- **Two DB-level defaults remain optional follow-ups** (not blocking): the durable "Default GridLayout" pinned record and the backend `rfq_detail` `col_order`. Both are now achieved in code, so the DB versions are only "tidy long-term" niceties.
- **No PR opened.** AGENTS.md asks to keep an `albert-2sandbox → develop` PR updated — say the word and I'll create/update it (it would not touch `main`).

A couple of things I couldn't verify from code alone and would confirm in the running app: the exact live `status` values (the pill maps `active/draft/inactive`, everything else → neutral) and that the detail grid's live colIds are `name/price/note` (the override map also covers the `quote_price/vendor_note` aliases defensively).
